title: GPS — Writing Skill
description: Build paragraphs using GPS structure. Assemble sentences into correct PEEEL/UPEPE order.

paragraph_assembly.title: PEEEL — Opinion Essay (Zoos)

sentences.text: Although many zoos have made great improvements, there can be no doubt that captive animals suffer.
sentences.label: Point

sentences.text: Not only are the enclosures usually far too small, but the climate and conditions are often completely unsuitable.
sentences.label: Explanation

sentences.text: Polar bears, for instance, travel across vast distances daily and yet, in some tropical regions, they are kept in small pens.
sentences.label: Example

sentences.text: In addition to this discomfort, zoo animals can suffer stress as a result of unfamiliar cage-mates, not to mention noisy visitors.
sentences.label: Effect

sentences.text: Clearly, the welfare of these animals is severely compromised.
sentences.label: Link

paragraph_assembly.failTip: Remember: Point (claim) → Explanation (why/how) → Example (specific proof) → Effect (consequence) → Link (back to thesis).

paragraph_assembly.title: PEEEL — Problem-Solution (Crime)

sentences.text: Despite the seriousness of the reoffending problem, there are a number of possible ways to remedy the situation.
sentences.label: Point

sentences.text: The key factor is the treatment of the underlying issues.
sentences.label: Explanation

sentences.text: In many cases, this may mean addiction rehabilitation in prison or in the community.
sentences.label: Example

sentences.text: Providing education programs or training can enhance employability, which would dramatically reduce their risk of reoffending.
sentences.label: Effect

sentences.text: Clearly, addressing root causes is more effective than punishment alone.
sentences.label: Link

paragraph_assembly.failTip: The Explanation tells us WHAT the key factor is. The Example gives a specific case. The Effect shows the RESULT of that action.

paragraph_assembly.title: UPEPE — Causes (Childhood Obesity)

sentences.text: There are two main causes of weight problems among children.
sentences.label: Umbrella

sentences.text: The first one is poor diet.
sentences.label: Point 1

sentences.text: Indeed, youngsters today consume vast quantities of fat, sugar and salt in fast food, which inevitably leads to weight gain.
sentences.label: Extend 1 (explain + evidence)

sentences.text: The other major factor is sedentary lifestyles.
sentences.label: Point 2

sentences.text: Modern children tend to spend their day sitting in front of screens, meaning they cannot burn the calories they consume.
sentences.label: Extend 2 (explain + effect)

paragraph_assembly.failTip: UPEPE order: Umbrella (covers both) → Point 1 → Extend 1 (explain + prove it) → Point 2 → Extend 2 (explain + prove it). Each Extend does DOUBLE DUTY — it explains WHY and gives evidence.

paragraph_assembly.title: UPEPE — Solutions (School Behaviour)

sentences.text: Clearly, in order to manage the problem, it must be tackled both at school and at home.
sentences.label: Umbrella

sentences.text: On an institutional level, school authorities need to get tough on bad behaviour.
sentences.label: Point 1

sentences.text: That means they need to set rules regarding uniforms, homework, and classroom conduct, with consequences like detention if broken.
sentences.label: Extend 1 (explain + example)

sentences.text: On the home front, parents too need to step up.
sentences.label: Point 2

sentences.text: Without expectations and limitations around behaviour, they are setting their child up for a life of trouble.
sentences.label: Extend 2 (explain + effect)

paragraph_assembly.failTip: Notice how 'On an institutional level' and 'On the home front' signal the TWO solutions. Each Extend gives concrete detail about HOW that solution works.

paragraph_assembly.title: Introduction — Discussion Essay (Art Funding)

sentences.text: It is generally accepted that art enriches a society and plays a valuable role in its culture and history.
sentences.label: Hook

sentences.text: Although some support government investment in galleries and artistic organisations, others feel that this money should be spent on public services.
sentences.label: Background

sentences.text: In this essay, I will consider both views and explain why I agree that state funding of the arts should be redirected.
sentences.label: Thesis

paragraph_assembly.failTip: Introduction order is always: Hook (general topic) → Background (the debate/narrowing) → Thesis (your plan/position). The Hook is the widest; the Thesis is the most specific.

paragraph_assembly.title: Conclusion — Opinion Essay (Space)

sentences.text: In conclusion, humanity's thirst for knowledge is unquenchable.
sentences.label: Signal + Restatement

sentences.text: It is time, however, for private corporations to invest.
sentences.label: Concession + Shift

sentences.text: Governments must take responsibility for their real duty here on earth.
sentences.label: Final Position

paragraph_assembly.failTip: Conclusion order: Signal ('In conclusion') → Concession (acknowledge other side) → Final Position (your strongest statement). Always end on YOUR position, not the concession.

slots.label: P — Point
slots.tpl: Perhaps the most _____ regarding children's social media use is the impact on their mental health.

slots.label: E — Explanation
slots.tpl: This _____ the constant comparison with peers' curated lives online, which triggers anxiety.

slots.label: E — Example
slots.tpl: Indeed, research found that _____ teenagers on social media reported symptoms of depression.

slots.label: E — Effect
slots.tpl: If left unaddressed, this _____ young people's self-esteem and academic performance.

slots.label: L — Link
slots.tpl: Clearly, protecting children from these harmful effects is _____.

gps_builder.title: UPEPE — Causes (Problem-Solution)
gps_builder.topic: "What are the causes of childhood obesity?"

slots.label: U — Umbrella
slots.tpl: There are two _____ of weight problems among children.

slots.label: P1 — Point 1
slots.tpl: The first one is _____.

slots.label: E1 — Extend 1
slots.tpl: Youngsters consume vast quantities of fat and sugar, which inevitably _____ weight gain.

slots.label: P2 — Point 2
slots.tpl: The other major factor is _____.

slots.label: E2 — Extend 2
slots.tpl: Modern children spend their day in front of screens, _____ them being unable to burn calories.

gps_builder.title: PEEEL — Discussion Essay (Reporter Mode)
gps_builder.topic: "Some feel zoos should be closed. Discuss both views."

slots.label: P — Point (Reporter)
slots.tpl: _____ that they serve an indispensable role in conservation.

slots.label: E — Explanation
slots.tpl: Breeding rare species in captivity _____ the survival of endangered populations.

slots.label: E — Example
slots.tpl: Indeed, _____ have successfully reintroduced pandas and condors into the wild.

slots.label: E — Effect
slots.tpl: Without these programs, many species would face extinction rather than _____ from conservation.

slots.label: L — Link
slots.tpl: Clearly, the role of zoos in species preservation is _____.

slots.label: U — Umbrella
slots.tpl: There are several _____ that can be taken to address this issue.

slots.label: P1 — Point 1
slots.tpl: Firstly, governments could _____ apprenticeship programmes in key industries.

slots.label: E1 — Extend 1
slots.tpl: This would _____ young graduates' practical skills and make them more attractive to employers.

slots.label: P2 — Point 2
slots.tpl: In addition, authorities could _____ companies that discriminate based on age.

slots.label: E2 — Extend 2
slots.tpl: Such legislation would _____ the disadvantages young people face when competing with experienced candidates.

slots.label: E — Effect (adverb)
slots.tpl: Without practical training, graduates would _____ struggle to compete.

slots.label: E — Effect (noun)
slots.tpl: This could have _____ on both their careers and the economy.

slots.label: L — Link (adjective)
slots.tpl: Clearly, curriculum reform is _____.

shared_vs_specialist.ph: leads to / stems from / contributes to
shared_vs_specialist.tip: Causation verbs work in ALL 5 essay types. Highest-ROI items in the bank.
Impact nouns are universal — every essay type needs Effect sentences.
Evaluative adjectives work everywhere: Points, Links, Conclusions.
Intensity adverbs amplify any claim in any essay type.

shared_vs_specialist.ph: a pervasive problem / a growing concern
shared_vs_specialist.tip: Problem nouns are universal. Every essay type can name problems.
Change verbs work everywhere. They show HOW things get better or worse.

shared_vs_specialist.ph: the vast majority / a growing number / individuals
shared_vs_specialist.tip: Quantifiers and academic people-words are universal. Every essay type needs hedging and formal register.

shared_vs_specialist.ph: X faces challenges / X poses risks / X suffers from
shared_vs_specialist.tip: Existence verbs are universal. "Poses risks" appears in ALL 5 types in the Shareability Matrix.

shared_vs_specialist.ph: it is claimed / supporters argue / it is felt
shared_vs_specialist.tip: Reporter verbs are ONLY for Discussion essays — reporting others' views neutrally. In Opinion, you state YOUR view. In P-S, you describe objectively.
Reporter people-nouns name WHO holds each view. Only Discussion essays present named groups of supporters vs. opponents.
The Shareability Matrix shows these in Opinion ✅, P-S ✅, and Two-Part ✅. NOT in Discussion or A-D. They prescribe actions — Opinion essays assert change, P-S essays propose solutions.
Funding verbs are P-S specialists (+Two-Part). They name specific financial actions. You wouldn't "subsidise" in a Discussion or Opinion essay.
The matrix shows "far outweigh" in Opinion ✅ and A-D ✅. It compares two sides on a scale — used in Opinion conclusions and A-D "outweigh" questions.

shared_vs_specialist.ph: a further plus / the downside / a serious drawback
shared_vs_specialist.tip: These nouns FRAME the discussion as advantages vs. disadvantages. "The downside" only makes sense when you've discussed the upside.
Strong assertive language expressing YOUR position. Discussion uses reporter verbs; P-S stays more objective with "should" not "ought to be."

shared_vs_specialist.ph: legitimate concerns / the diversion of funds
shared_vs_specialist.tip: The matrix shows these in Opinion ✅, Discussion ✅, and A-D ✅. They evaluate negatives — useful whenever you judge a downside, regardless of essay structure.
